content:
Excessive AI use is causing a new form of mental exhaustion called "AI brain fry," particularly among high-performing employees pushing beyond their limits. A Harvard Business Review study of 1,500 workers shows AI is increasing workloads and mental fatigue instead of reducing them.

• AI use leads to constant task-switching and oversight, creating cognitive overload and "mental fog" in workers.  
• 14% of full-time employees already experience this fatigue, especially in tech, IT, and finance roles.  
• High oversight demands, like managing multiple AI agents, increase mental fatigue by 12% and decision fatigue by 33%.  
• This burnout raises employee turnover risk by 10% and costs firms millions due to poor or delayed decisions.
